onboarding.title: Welcome to Brain Vault
onboarding.subtitle: Your personal knowledge base for capturing and connecting ideas
onboarding.sampleDataDescription: We've added 5 sample notes (journal, meeting notes, book reviews) and 4 tags to show you how it works.

steps.title: Capture everything
steps.description: Create notes for journals, meetings, book reviews, research, or anything else. Write in Markdown.

steps.title: Connect your knowledge
steps.description: Link related notes together and use tags to organize by topic. Build your personal knowledge graph.

steps.title: Use your AI agent
steps.description: Ask your agent to create notes, summarize ideas, or find connections between your entries.

reference.overview: A Markdown-native knowledge base for capturing daily journals, meeting notes, book reviews, and research. Link notes together and organize with tags and note types.

concepts.name: Notes
concepts.description: Individual knowledge entries with Markdown content. Types include daily journal, meeting notes, book review, project, research, and more.

concepts.name: Tags
concepts.description: Color-coded labels for organizing notes by topic — productivity, research, personal, work, etc.

prompts: Create a book review note for 'Atomic Habits' with a 9/10 rating
Write a daily journal entry for today
Link my meeting notes to the project plan note
tips: Notes support Markdown formatting — use headers, lists, and code blocks in the content field
Pin important notes by setting pinned: true so they appear at the top
Link notes together using the linkedNoteIds field to build a knowledge graph
Action items can be embedded in meeting notes with task, owner, and done fields

notes.title: No notes yet
notes.description: Start capturing your ideas, meeting notes, or journal entries. Every piece of knowledge has a home here.
notes.suggestedPrompt: Create a daily journal entry for today — what I'm working on and key decisions
